network-outage.title: Possible network outage in your area
network-outage.description: Your signal loss appears to be related to a network issue in your area rather than your device.

fixSteps.title: Check your network provider status page
fixSteps.detail: Visit your network provider's website or app and look for any reported outages in your area.

fixSteps.title: Toggle airplane mode on and off
fixSteps.detail: Swipe down from the top of your screen, turn on airplane mode for 30 seconds, then turn it off again. This forces your phone to reconnect to the network.

fixSteps.title: Restart your phone
fixSteps.detail: A full restart can clear network faults. Hold the power button and select restart.

fixSteps.title: Move to a different location
fixSteps.detail: If possible, move to a different area. If signal returns elsewhere, the issue is likely localised to that area.

sim-issue.title: SIM card or account issue
sim-issue.description: Your issue may be related to your SIM card not being read correctly or an account problem with your provider.

fixSteps.title: Remove and reinsert your SIM card
fixSteps.detail: Power off your phone, carefully remove the SIM card using the SIM tray tool, wait 10 seconds, reinsert it and power back on.

fixSteps.title: Check your account is active
fixSteps.detail: Log into your provider's app or website to confirm your account is active and your plan has not expired.

fixSteps.title: Test your SIM in another phone
fixSteps.detail: If possible, put your SIM into another phone to check if the problem follows the SIM or stays with your device.

fixSteps.title: Contact your network provider
fixSteps.detail: If none of the above work, contact your provider directly. They can check if your SIM needs to be replaced or if there is an account issue.

weak-coverage.title: Weak signal coverage in your area
weak-coverage.description: Your location may have limited mobile coverage. This can be caused by distance from a mast, building materials or geography.

fixSteps.title: Move to a window or go outside
fixSteps.detail: Building materials like concrete and metal block signals. Moving closer to a window or stepping outside can improve signal strength significantly.

fixSteps.title: Check your network mode settings
fixSteps.detail: Go to Settings → Mobile Network → Network Mode. Try switching between 4G and 3G to see if one gives a stronger signal in your area.

fixSteps.title: Check coverage for your area online
fixSteps.detail: Visit your network provider's coverage checker and enter your location to see what level of coverage is expected in your area.

fixSteps.title: Consider a signal booster or Wi-Fi calling
fixSteps.detail: If you are in a known weak area, enabling Wi-Fi calling allows calls and texts over your Wi-Fi connection instead of the mobile network.

data-congestion.title: Mobile data congestion or speed issue
data-congestion.description: Your mobile data may be slow due to network congestion, data limits or APN settings on your device.

fixSteps.title: Check your data allowance
fixSteps.detail: Log into your provider's app and check if you have used up your monthly data allowance. Some providers reduce speed after a certain limit.

fixSteps.title: Toggle airplane mode on and off
fixSteps.detail: This forces your phone to reconnect to the network and can refresh your data connection.

fixSteps.title: Check your APN settings
fixSteps.detail: Go to Settings → Mobile Network → Access Point Names. Make sure the APN settings match your provider's recommended settings which can be found on their support page.

fixSteps.title: Try a different location
fixSteps.detail: Network congestion is more common in busy areas. If you are in a crowded location, moving slightly can sometimes give a better connection.

network-interference.title: Network interference causing signal drops
network-interference.description: Your signal may be dropping due to interference from nearby objects or because your phone is switching between network masts.

fixSteps.title: Toggle airplane mode on and off
fixSteps.detail: This forces your phone to reconnect to the nearest strongest mast and can stop it switching between masts repeatedly.

fixSteps.title: Manually select your network
fixSteps.detail: Go to Settings → Mobile Network → Network Operators → Search manually. Select your provider from the list. This stops automatic switching.

fixSteps.title: Restart your phone
fixSteps.detail: A restart can clear the network connection cache and help your phone find a stable connection.

low-storage.title: Low storage causing performance issues
low-storage.description: Your phone needs free storage space to run smoothly. When storage is nearly full, performance can drop significantly.

fixSteps.title: Check your available storage
fixSteps.detail: Go to Settings → Storage to see how much space is used and available.

fixSteps.title: Delete unused apps
fixSteps.detail: Go through your apps and uninstall any you no longer use. Even freeing up 1-2GB can make a noticeable difference.

fixSteps.title: Clear app caches
fixSteps.detail: Go to Settings → Apps, select each large app and tap Clear Cache. This removes temporary files without deleting your data.

fixSteps.title: Move photos and videos to cloud storage
fixSteps.detail: Use Google Photos or iCloud to back up and remove media from your device. Photos and videos are usually the biggest use of storage.

too-many-background-apps.title: Too many apps running in the background
too-many-background-apps.description: Apps running in the background use memory and processing power which can slow your phone down noticeably.

fixSteps.title: Close all background apps
fixSteps.detail: Press the recent apps button and swipe away all open apps. This frees up RAM immediately.

fixSteps.title: Restrict background activity for apps
fixSteps.detail: Go to Settings → Apps → select an app → Battery → Restrict background activity. Do this for apps you do not need running in the background.

fixSteps.title: Restart your phone
fixSteps.detail: A restart closes all background processes and gives your phone a fresh start.

software-fault.title: Software or update causing performance issues
software-fault.description: A recent software update or app installation may have introduced a conflict or bug that is affecting performance.

fixSteps.title: Restart your phone
fixSteps.detail: A restart can clear temporary software faults. Hold the power button and select restart.

fixSteps.title: Check for further software updates
fixSteps.detail: Go to Settings → Software Update. Sometimes a follow-up patch is released to fix issues introduced by a previous update.

fixSteps.title: Identify the problem app
fixSteps.detail: If the issue started after installing a specific app, try uninstalling it to see if performance improves.

fixSteps.title: Clear the system cache partition
fixSteps.detail: On Android, this can be done through recovery mode. Search for the specific steps for your phone model. This does not delete your data.

old-hardware.title: Ageing hardware limitations
old-hardware.description: Older phones may struggle with newer apps and software updates as the hardware becomes less capable of keeping up with demands.

fixSteps.title: Reduce animations and visual effects
fixSteps.detail: Go to Settings → Developer Options → Window animation scale and set it to 0.5x. This makes the phone feel noticeably faster.

fixSteps.title: Use lite versions of apps where available
fixSteps.detail: Many popular apps like Facebook and Google Maps have lite versions that use less memory and processing power.

fixSteps.title: Perform a factory reset as a last resort
fixSteps.detail: A factory reset removes all data and restores the phone to its original state. Back up everything first. This often significantly improves performance on older devices.

battery-draining-app.title: An app is draining your battery
battery-draining-app.description: One or more apps are using excessive battery in the background causing your battery to drain faster than expected.

fixSteps.title: Check battery usage by app
fixSteps.detail: Go to Settings → Battery → Battery Usage. Look for any app using an unusually high percentage of battery.

fixSteps.title: Restrict background activity for the problem app
fixSteps.detail: Go to Settings → Apps → select the app → Battery → Restrict background activity.

fixSteps.title: Uninstall the app if not needed
fixSteps.detail: If the problem app is not essential, uninstalling it is the most effective fix.

fixSteps.title: Check for an app update
fixSteps.detail: Sometimes battery drain is caused by a bug in a specific version of an app. Updating it may resolve the issue.

overheating-app.title: An app is causing your phone to overheat
overheating-app.description: Certain apps particularly games or streaming apps can cause your processor to work very hard leading to overheating.

fixSteps.title: Close the app and let your phone cool down
fixSteps.detail: Remove the case if you have one and place the phone on a flat cool surface for 10 minutes.

fixSteps.title: Check which app is causing the heat
fixSteps.detail: Go to Settings → Battery → Battery Usage and look for apps using a high percentage. Heavy apps like games are common culprits.

fixSteps.title: Reduce screen brightness and close background apps
fixSteps.detail: High brightness combined with multiple background apps puts extra load on the processor and battery.

fixSteps.title: Check for app updates
fixSteps.detail: An app update may include optimisations that reduce processor usage.

faulty-charger.title: Faulty charger or charging port issue
faulty-charger.description: Your charging problem may be caused by a damaged cable, faulty charger or debris in the charging port.

fixSteps.title: Try a different charging cable and adapter
fixSteps.detail: Test with a different cable and charger to rule out a faulty accessory. Always use a certified charger for your device.

fixSteps.title: Clean the charging port
fixSteps.detail: Use a soft dry toothbrush or compressed air to gently clean any dust or debris from the charging port. Do not use metal objects.

fixSteps.title: Try wireless charging if supported
fixSteps.detail: If your phone supports wireless charging, try this to determine if the charging port itself is the problem.

fixSteps.title: Visit a repair centre
fixSteps.detail: If none of the above work, the charging port may need professional repair or replacement.

old-battery.title: Battery degradation over time
old-battery.description: Phone batteries degrade with use and age. After 2 years a battery may only hold 80 percent of its original charge capacity.

fixSteps.title: Check your battery health
fixSteps.detail: On iPhone go to Settings → Battery → Battery Health. On Android the option varies by manufacturer but can often be found in Settings → Battery.

fixSteps.title: Enable battery saver mode
fixSteps.detail: Go to Settings → Battery → Battery Saver. This limits background activity and reduces power consumption to extend battery life.

fixSteps.title: Reduce screen brightness and timeout
fixSteps.detail: The screen is the biggest drain on most phones. Reduce brightness and set the screen to turn off after 30 seconds of inactivity.

fixSteps.title: Consider a battery replacement
fixSteps.detail: If battery health is below 80 percent, a battery replacement is the most effective long-term solution. Contact your manufacturer or a certified repair centre.

corrupted-app-data.title: Corrupted app data or cache
corrupted-app-data.description: The app may have corrupted temporary files or data that is causing it to crash or behave unexpectedly.

fixSteps.title: Clear the app cache
fixSteps.detail: Go to Settings → Apps → select the app → Storage → Clear Cache. This removes temporary files without deleting your personal data in the app.

fixSteps.title: Clear the app data if cache clear did not work
fixSteps.detail: Go to Settings → Apps → select the app → Storage → Clear Data. Warning: this will reset the app to its default state and you may need to log in again.

fixSteps.title: Uninstall and reinstall the app
fixSteps.detail: Uninstalling and reinstalling gives the app a completely fresh installation which usually resolves persistent crashing issues.

fixSteps.title: Check for an app update
fixSteps.detail: Open the Play Store or App Store and check if there is an update available for the app. Updates often fix crash bugs.

software-conflict.title: Software conflict after a system update
software-conflict.description: A recent system update may have caused a conflict with one or more of your apps leading to crashes or slow performance.

fixSteps.title: Restart your phone
fixSteps.detail: A restart after an update allows the system to complete any background processes and can resolve minor conflicts.

fixSteps.title: Update all your apps
fixSteps.detail: Open the Play Store or App Store and update all apps. Developers often release updates after a major OS update to fix compatibility issues.

fixSteps.title: Clear cache for affected apps
fixSteps.detail: Go to Settings → Apps → select the app → Storage → Clear Cache.

fixSteps.title: Check for a follow-up system update
fixSteps.detail: Go to Settings → Software Update. A follow-up patch may already be available to fix the issue.

router-issue.title: Router or home network issue
router-issue.description: The problem appears to be with your router or home network rather than your phone itself.

fixSteps.title: Restart your router
fixSteps.detail: Unplug your router from the power socket, wait 30 seconds and plug it back in. Wait 2 minutes for it to fully restart before testing.

fixSteps.title: Move closer to the router
fixSteps.detail: Wi-Fi signal weakens with distance and through walls. Test the connection when standing close to the router to rule out a range issue.

fixSteps.title: Check if other devices have the same problem
fixSteps.detail: If other devices also have no internet it confirms the issue is with the router or your broadband connection rather than your phone.

fixSteps.title: Contact your broadband provider
fixSteps.detail: If restarting the router does not resolve the issue your broadband provider may have a line fault or outage in your area.

saved-network-fault.title: Saved network settings causing connection issues
saved-network-fault.description: Your phone may have saved incorrect or outdated network settings for this Wi-Fi network which is preventing a proper connection.

fixSteps.title: Forget the network and reconnect
fixSteps.detail: Go to Settings → Wi-Fi, press and hold the network name, select Forget. Then reconnect by selecting the network and entering the password again.

fixSteps.title: Reset network settings
fixSteps.detail: Go to Settings → General Management → Reset → Reset Network Settings. This clears all saved Wi-Fi passwords and Bluetooth pairings so only do this if other steps have not worked.

fixSteps.title: Check the Wi-Fi password is correct
fixSteps.detail: Confirm you are using the correct password for the network. The password is usually on a label on the back of the router.

bluetooth-interference.title: Bluetooth interference affecting connectivity
bluetooth-interference.description: Bluetooth and Wi-Fi both use a specific frequency band and can interfere with each other causing connectivity problems.

fixSteps.title: Turn Bluetooth off and test Wi-Fi
fixSteps.detail: Go to Settings → Bluetooth and toggle it off. Check if your Wi-Fi connection improves. If it does, interference is the cause.

fixSteps.title: Switch your router to the 5GHz band
fixSteps.detail: If your router supports dual band, connect to the 5GHz network instead of 2.4GHz. The 5GHz band does not interfere with Bluetooth.

fixSteps.title: Move Bluetooth devices away from your router
fixSteps.detail: Keep Bluetooth speakers, headphones and other devices away from your router to reduce interference.
